title: Formula Guide & Examples
description: Learn how to write powerful formulas to calculate time, differences, and custom logic.

sections.title: Rules & Basics
sections.summary: Core concepts for writing formulas in this application.

subsections.title: Time Parsing & Minutes
subsections.summary: How time values are handled internally.
notes: All referenced time cells (e.g., [entrance], [exit]) are automatically converted to total minutes before evaluation.
Empty or invalid cell values become 0 during evaluation.

subsections.title: Cell References
subsections.summary: How to refer to other columns and rows.
notes: Use [columnKey] to reference the value of a column in the current row (e.g., [entrance], [exit]).
Use [PREV_CELL] to reference the value of the SAME column in the PREVIOUS row. Useful for running totals or cumulative sums.
Constants defined in the settings can be used directly by typing their name (e.g., hourlyRate).

subsections.title: Output Formatting
subsections.summary: Controlling how the result is displayed.
notes: The default output format is NUMBER.
Use TIME(expression) to force the final result to be formatted as a time string (e.g., 'HH:MM').
Use NUM(expression) to force the result to be a number.
Use STRING(expression) to force the result to be a string.
The 'Keep Empty if Negative' column setting will blank the cell if the final numeric result is less than 0 (applies to NUMBER and TIME outputs).

subsections.title: Time Literals
subsections.summary: Writing time values directly in formulas.
notes: You can write time values directly using wrapper functions without quotes.
TIME(8:30) represents the time 8:30 (evaluates to 510 minutes).

sections.title: Operators
notes: Comparison: == (equal), != (not equal), < (less than), > (greater than), <= (less or equal), >= (greater or equal)

sections.title: Available Functions
sections.summary: Built-in functions you can use in your formulas.

items.name: IF
items.summary: Returns trueValue if condition is true, otherwise returns falseValue.

items.name: TIME
items.summary: Forces the output to be formatted as time (HH:MM). Also used to write time literals like TIME(8:30).

items.name: HOURS
items.summary: Converts a time literal or number into hours. E.g., HOURS(8:30) becomes 8.5. HOURS(120) becomes 2.

items.name: MINS
items.summary: Parses a time literal into minutes, or keeps a number as minutes. E.g., MINS(1:30) becomes 90.

items.name: NUM
items.summary: Forces the output to be formatted as a number.

items.name: STRING
items.summary: Forces the output to be formatted as a string.

items.name: MAX
items.summary: Returns the largest of the given values.

items.name: MIN
items.summary: Returns the smallest of the given values.

items.name: ROUND
items.summary: Rounds a number to the nearest integer.

items.name: FLOOR
items.summary: Rounds a number down to the nearest integer.

items.name: CEIL
items.summary: Rounds a number up to the nearest integer.

items.name: ABS
items.summary: Returns the absolute value of a number.

items.name: AND
items.summary: Returns TRUE if all conditions are true. Can also use && operator.

items.name: OR
items.summary: Returns TRUE if at least one condition is true. Can also use || operator.

items.name: NOT
items.summary: Returns the opposite of the condition. Can also use ! operator.

sections.title: Examples & Use Cases
sections.summary: Comprehensive examples showing how to combine functions and operators.

examples.label: Simple Duration
examples.note: Calculates the time difference between exit and entrance, formatted as HH:MM.

examples.label: Duration minus Lunch
examples.note: Calculates total worked time by subtracting the lunch break duration.

examples.label: Convert to Decimal Hours
examples.note: Converts total minutes into decimal hours (e.g., 8:30 becomes 8.5).

examples.label: Night Shift (Crossing Midnight)
examples.note: Correctly calculates duration if the shift starts before midnight and ends after midnight.

examples.label: Auto-deduct 30m Lunch (if > 6h)
examples.note: Automatically subtracts 30 minutes if the total time worked exceeds 6 hours.

examples.label: Cap Maximum Hours at 12
examples.note: Ensures the calculated time never exceeds 12 hours.

examples.label: Overtime (after 8 hours)
examples.note: Calculates any time worked beyond 8 hours. Returns 0:00 if less than 8 hours.

examples.label: Regular Time (capped at 8 hours)
examples.note: Calculates regular hours worked, capped at a maximum of 8 hours.

examples.label: Check if Late
examples.note: Returns 'Late' if the entrance time is after 9:00 AM.

examples.label: Status using Ternary Operator
examples.note: A shorter way to write an IF statement using the ? : syntax.

examples.label: Flag Missing Punches
examples.note: Checks if either entrance or exit is empty (evaluates to 0).

examples.label: Cumulative Sum (Running Total)
examples.note: Adds the current row's decimal hours to the previous row's total. Requires the column to be evaluated sequentially.
